description_long: This is a rectangular outdoor air conditioning unit, primarily metallic in construction, with a large circular fan protected by a wire grille on the front face. The unit features a mesh-like exhaust grille on the back and a series of horizontal vents on one side, likely for airflow management. Prominent branding and certification stickers, including a GREE logo and a USAID emblem, are visible on the front panel. Two copper pipes, one insulated, extend from the side, indicating refrigerant connections for installation. The casing is mostly a matte gray metal, with some plastic and rubber components visible around the piping. The overall design is robust and functional, suitable for mounting on exterior walls in urban environments.
description: A robust, rectangular outdoor air conditioner unit with a large front fan grille, side vents, and visible copper piping. Prominent branding and certification stickers are present on the metallic casing.
description_view_0: Side view showing the insulated copper piping and a small access panel.
description_view_1: Rear view displaying a large mesh grille for exhaust airflow.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with horizontal vent slats for air intake or exhaust.
description_view_3: Front view featuring a large circular fan grille, GREE branding, and certification stickers.
attribute_brand_logo: A circular GREE logo in blue and red with the word 'GREE' in blue, located on the front panel along with a USAID emblem and certification stickers.
attribute_grille_pattern: Front grille is circular with concentric wire rings and radial spokes; rear grille is a dense mesh pattern; side grille has horizontal slats.
attribute_exhaust_vent_design: Rear mesh grille serves as the main exhaust vent, rectangular in shape and spanning most of the back panel.
